reasoning: `seq_splice` is `data.take(pos) + v + data.skip(pos + v.len())`. Length = pos + v.len() + (data.len() - pos - v.len()) = data.len(). Correct by sequence arithmetic.
With empty `v`, `data.take(pos) + Seq::empty() + data.skip(pos)` equals `data.take(pos) + data.skip(pos)` which equals `data`. Correct by definition.
Index `pos + k` falls in the middle segment `v` of the concatenation `take(pos) + v + skip(...)`. The element at that position is `v[k]`. Correct by sequence indexing.
Index `k < pos` falls in the first segment `data.take(pos)`, which preserves original elements. Correct by `take` semantics.
Index `k >= pos + v.len()` falls in the third segment `data.skip(pos + v.len())`, which preserves original elements at those positions. Correct by `skip` semantics.
